zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Vere於1234年出生在英國奧可福爾時，她的父親Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford27歲，母親Countess Hawise de Quincy50歲。她於1256年與Hugh de Cressy在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1341年之後在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾，埋葬在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Vere was born in 1234, in Oxfordshire, England, her father, Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford, was 27 and her mother, Countess Hawise de Quincy, was 50. She married Hugh de Cressy in 1256, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died after 1341, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Vere est née en 1234, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Countess Hawise de Quincy, avait 50 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh de Cressy en 1256, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée après 1341, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Vere 1234 in Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford 27 und ihre Mutter Countess Hawise de Quincy 50. Sie hat 1256 in Essex, England Hugh de Cressy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb nach 1341 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Vereが1234年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxfordは27歳，母Countess Hawise de Quincyは50歳であった。1256年にイギリスエセックスでHugh de Cressyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1341年以後にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアで死去し，イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Vere(이)가 1234년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Countess Hawise de Quincy(은)는 50세였다. 그녀는 1256년 영국 에세스에서 Hugh de Cressy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1341년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Vere родилась в 1234 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Countess Hawise de Quincy, 50. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Cressy в 1256 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла после 1341 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
